title: joist
text: joist: a horizontal structural member that transfers load from flooring or a deck to beams, typically running parallel and spaced at a regular interval such as 16 in on center.

title: wall stud
text: wall stud: a vertical framing member in a wall, smaller in cross section than a post, spaced 16 in or 24 in on center to carry sheathing and load.

title: rafter
text: rafter: a sloped structural roof member running from the ridge or hip down to the wall plate to support the roof deck.

title: soffit
text: soffit: the finished underside of a building element such as a roof overhang, eave, cornice, or stair.

title: truss
text: truss: a rigid framework of two-force members forming triangles, commonly used to span roofs and floors efficiently.

title: lintel
text: lintel: a horizontal structural member spanning the space between two supports above an opening such as a door or window; in wood framing it is called a header.

title: girder
text: girder: a large primary support beam that carries the load of smaller beams or joists framing into it.

title: gypsum drywall
text: gypsum drywall: an interior panel of calcium sulfate dihydrate (gypsum) faced with paper, fastened to framing to form walls and ceilings.

title: plywood
text: plywood: a manufactured wood panel made of thin veneers glued with alternating grain, used for sheathing, subflooring, and roof decking.

title: sill plate
text: sill plate: the bottom horizontal member of a wall, anchored to the foundation, to which the vertical studs attach.

title: purlin
text: purlin: a horizontal roof member running perpendicular to rafters or trusses to support the roof deck or reduce rafter span.

title: reinforcing bar (rebar)
text: reinforcing bar or rebar: a steel bar or mesh embedded in concrete to carry tensile forces the concrete cannot.

title: formwork
text: formwork: the temporary mold into which concrete is poured and held until it cures and can support itself.

title: wall plate (top plate)
text: wall plate: a horizontal load-bearing member at the top of a wall (the top plate) that ties studs together and carries the roof or floor above.

title: roof shingle
text: roof shingle: an overlapping plate of asphalt, wood, or other material laid in courses to shed water off a sloped roof.

title: mortar
text: mortar: a workable paste of cement, lime, sand, and water that binds masonry units such as brick and block together.

title: concrete
text: concrete: a composite of cement, water, and aggregate that cures into a strong, hard material used for footings, slabs, and walls.

title: gable
text: gable: the generally triangular portion of a wall between the sloping edges of a dual-pitched roof.

title: rain gutter
text: rain gutter: a channel at the roof eave that collects runoff and directs it to downspouts away from the foundation.
